mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate rises above 5.0%, the cost of capital increases for firms, reducing investment and lowering equity valuations, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: If the mean 20-day forward return in periods with FEDFUNDS > 5% is not negative and statistically different from the mean in other periods (p < 0.10, one-tailed t-test), reject.

mechanism_narrative: A declining unemployment rate tightens labor markets, pressuring corporate margins and foreshadowing rate hikes, which depresses equity prices; the effect is persistent and always operative across business cycles.
failure_condition: If the Spearman rank correlation between the monthly change in UNRATE and the subsequent 60-day S&P 500 return is greater than 0 over a rolling 3-year window, reject.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in headline inflation and the federal funds rate create a stagflationary environment that erodes real earnings and raises discount rates, causing sustained negative equity returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: If in periods where both conditions hold, the average 20-day forward S&P 500 return is not significantly negative (t-test p < 0.10) or the proportion of negative returns is less than 60%, reject.

mechanism_narrative: Rising housing starts signal robust residential investment and consumer confidence, leading to stronger economic growth and positive equity returns; this signal is always-on and does not require a threshold.
failure_condition: If the Spearman rank correlation between the monthly change in HOUST and the subsequent 20-day S&P 500 return is insignificant (p > 0.05) over a 5-year rolling window, reject.

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in industrial production over three months indicates softening aggregate supply and outright recession risk, which immediately depresses forward equity returns over a one-quarter horizon.
failure_condition: If the cumulative 60-day return after trigger is positive in more than 50% of cases, or the average return is not significantly less than zero (t-test p > 0.10), reject.

mechanism_narrative: In a late-cycle expansion marked by decelerating GDP and falling housing starts, an unemployment rate at or below 5% signals an overheating economy vulnerable to a downturn; this combination foreshadows negative equity returns over 20 days.
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with tight labor market
failure_condition: If in triggered periods the 20-day forward return is positive more than 40% of the time, or the mean return is not negative and statistically distinguishable from zero at 10% level, reject.

mechanism_narrative: An acceleration in retail sales growth above 2% over three months indicates strong consumer demand that boosts corporate revenues and equity prices, producing short-term positive returns within a week.
failure_condition: If the average 5-day forward return after trigger is not positive, or if the proportion of positive returns is less than 55%, reject.

mechanism_narrative: A two-month sequential decline in industrial production reveals persistent weakness in the manufacturing sector that immediately reduces equity valuations, triggering negative short-term returns within five trading days.
failure_condition: If the 5-day return after a 2-month decline in INDPRO is not negative on average, or if less than 60% of such events have negative returns, reject.
